message.content:
Test file: src/claim-office.spec.ts
Implementation file: src/claim-office.ts
Failing test: "3 runes + 3 moonstones (two blocks) → 120 G base + 12 G first insurance + 5 G fee = 137 G"
Passing tests so far: 9

Current behavior: `calculateBasePremium` only handles rune blocks specifically. Now we need a general approach for components (rune, moonstone, ...) where ANY component type forming a group of exactly 3 gives 60 G base, otherwise 25 G per component.

This is the right time to generalize: identify which item types are "components" (rune, moonstone — both insured at 250 G / 25 G base premium). For each component type group in the items list, if size === 3 → 60 G; else size × 25 G.

Main items (sword, amulet, staff, potion) still use their individual base premium.

Implementation steps:
1. Define a COMPONENT_TYPES set: {"rune", "moonstone"} (or use a separate COMPONENT_BASE_PREMIUMS map).
2. Group items by type.
3. For each type group:
   - If type is a component:
     - If group.length === 3 → 60 G
     - Else → group.length * 25 G
   - Else (main item): sum BASE_PREMIUMS[type] for each item
4. Sum these to get policy base.

Then apply existing first-insurance + fee logic.

All previous tests must still pass:
1. Empty → 5
2. 1 sword → 115
3. 1 amulet → 71
4. 1 staff → 93
5. 1 potion → 49
6. 2 swords → 225
7. 1 rune → 33
8. 3 runes → 71
9. 7 runes → 198
10. 3 runes + 3 moonstones → 137 ← new

Run `pnpm test` to verify all 10 pass.

EXPERIMENT MODE: Run autonomously, return after completion.

content.text: I'll analyze the current implementation and make the minimal change to pass the new test.
Now I'll make the minimal generalization to handle components (rune and moonstone) with block discount.

content.text:
All 10 tests pass.

Green Phase Complete:
- Implementation: Generalized `calculateBasePremium` to handle all component types (rune + moonstone). Added `moonstone: 25` to `BASE_PREMIUMS`, introduced `COMPONENT_TYPES` set, renamed `RUNE_BLOCK_*` to `BLOCK_*`, added `groupByType` helper, and reworked `calculateBasePremium` to iterate type groups, applying the block discount when a component type has exactly 3 items.
- Result: All 10 tests pass (28 todo remaining).
- Approach: Minimal generalization driven by the new test — only the change required to support both `rune` and `moonstone` block detection. Main item types (sword, amulet, staff, potion) still use the per-item sum path.

Relevant files:
- /home/experimenter/experiments/runs/2026-05-17_03-23-26_claim-office-example-mapping_v7-hybrid-green-refactor_opus-4-7-no-thinking/src/claim-office.ts

Proceeding to Refactor phase.
